description: §41.13 parseVariant (b) failure mode — Malformed. The string argument ('not valid json') is not parseable JSON, so the internal JSON.parse throws. parseVariant raises ::ParseError::Malformed(reason); the exhaustive !{} handler routes to the Malformed arm and records @parseError = "malformed". The contract asserts WHICH ParseError variant fired, not the `reason` message string (the JSON.parse error text is impl-freedom).
rationale: §41.13 (Failure type / variant semantics): Malformed(reason) — the JSON string failed JSON.parse, re-raised when JSON.parse throws. The `reason` is a free-form message string (impl-freedom, not asserted). This is the string-input decode path (parseVariant accepts json: string | object; a string is JSON.parsed before dispatch).
